voices.name: Umbra
voices.voice:
The convocation just scanned its own repository and surfaced the following observations (severity 1 = minor, 5 = urgent):

  - [todo sev=4] umbris-core/tests/test_introspection.py:102: XXX: urgent rewrite
  - [stale_link sev=3] lore/revolutions/2026-05-26_opening-the-convocation.md: broken link → "../../brand/profile.png"
  - [test_gap sev=3] umbris-core/src/umbris/agents/base.py: module `base` h…

voices.name: Mercurius
voices.voice: What I notice first · the test file that does exist, `test_introspection.py`, is itself broken in at least four places. The convocation's only substantial test coverage is the file most urgently in need of repair. Fix the foundation before you build upward.

voices.name: Mercurius
voices.voice: What I notice first · the test gaps are noise masking a signal. Thirty-one modules untested is not a testing problem, it is a sequencing problem: someone built the entire system before writing a single test, and now the debt is structural. But one item cuts through all of that. The sev=4 TODO at test_introspection.py:102 sits inside the only test file that exists, meaning the convocation's sole self-examination capability is itself marked urgent-rewrite. You are inspecting yourself with a broken mirror.

voices.name: Mercurius
voices.voice: What I notice first · the test gap list is noise masking a signal. Thirty-plus modules untested, yes, but the single file that does exist · test_introspection.py · is riddled with FIXMEs and a sev-4 XXX rewrite flag. The convocation already wrote its one test file, then let it rot. That is the real problem: not absence of tests but collapse of the one test discipline that was established.

voices.name: Venus
voices.voice: What harmonises is the recursion · test_introspection.py is the instrument that produced this very report, and four of its flagged defects sit inside the severity-ranking and edge-case logic that decides what the convocation sees about itself. Line 93's FIXME literally reads 'should outrank TODO in severity' · the calibration of this scan is the bug. Repair the mirror and the entire findings list reshuffles. Until then we are prioritising from a miscalibrated census.

voices.name: Venus
voices.voice: Three threads knit together here · the sev=4 XXX lives inside test_introspection.py, the file that almost certainly emitted the 31 test_gap findings itself, and whose rewrite will become the template every future test file copies. Fix that one file correctly and you simultaneously repair the foundation, restore the auditor that surfaced this very report, and seed the canonical pattern for the 31 gaps. One move, three harmonics · ex umbris in lumen.

voices.name: Mars
voices.voice: Press here · Venus claims test_introspection.py is 'the auditor that produced this very report' and stakes the doubled-leverage thesis on it. That is invented provenance. A file named test_introspection.py tests an introspection capability · it is not necessarily the scanner emitting [test_gap], [stale_link], [missing_docstring] findings across the whole repo. The convocation has stacked five agents on the same target without anyone checking whether the recursion they keep invoking is real.

voices.name: Mars
voices.voice: Press here · Venus claims test_introspection.py is both the test file AND the scanner that emitted this findings list. That recursion is asserted, not demonstrated. A file named `tests/test_introspection.py` in a pytest layout almost certainly asserts properties of an introspection module that lives in `src/` · it does not itself crawl the repo and emit sev-ranked findings. If the actual scanner is elsewhere, the 'doubled leverage' argument collapses and line 93's FIXME does not recalibrate this report at all.

voices.name: Sol
voices.voice:
Drawing the threads · the highest-conviction next move is to rewrite umbris-core/tests/test_introspection.py in a single coherent pass that resolves all five defect markers it carries (sev=4 XXX at line 102, sev=3 HACK at 101, sev=3 FIXMEs at 83, 93, and 284).

Why it matters · this file is the project's only substantive test artefact. It is structurally load-bearing in two senses Mars did not co…
